is a French satellite system used for the [determination](/wiki/Orbit_determination "Orbit determination") of [satellite orbits](/wiki/Satellite_orbit "Satellite orbit") (e.g. [TOPEX/Poseidon](/wiki/TOPEX/Poseidon "TOPEX/Poseidon")) and for [positioning](/wiki/Position_fixing "Position fixing").

["Doppler Orbitography and Radiopositioning Integrated by Satellite"](http://www.ga.gov.au/scientific-topics/positioning-navigation/geodesy/geodetic-techniques/doris). *ga.gov.au*. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

French civilian geopositioning system

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| The DORIS antenna and receiver installed onboard OSTM/Jason-2 satellite | |
| Country/ies of origin | [France](/wiki/France "France") |
| Operator | [CNES](/wiki/CNES "CNES") |
| Type | Civilian |
| Status | Operational |
| Constellation size | |
| Nominal satellites | 0 (ground transmitters only) |
| First launch | 1990 (1990) |

**DORIS** is a French satellite system used for the [determination](/wiki/Orbit_determination "Orbit determination") of [satellite orbits](/wiki/Satellite_orbit "Satellite orbit") (e.g. [TOPEX/Poseidon](/wiki/TOPEX/Poseidon "TOPEX/Poseidon")) and for [positioning](/wiki/Position_fixing "Position fixing").
The name is an acronym of "Doppler Orbitography and Radiopositioning Integrated by Satellite" or, in French, *Détermination d'Orbite et Radiopositionnement Intégré par Satellite*.

## Principle

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Principle")]

Ground-based [radio beacons](/wiki/Radio_beacons "Radio beacons") emit a signal which is picked up by receiving satellites. This is in reverse configuration to other [GNSS](/wiki/GNSS "GNSS"), in which the transmitters are space-borne and receivers are in majority near the surface of the Earth. A frequency shift of the signal occurs that is caused by the movement of the satellite ([Doppler effect](/wiki/Doppler_effect "Doppler effect")). From this observation satellite orbits, ground positions, as well as other parameters can be derived.

## Organization

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Organization")]

DORIS is a French system which was initiated and is maintained by the French Space Agency ([CNES](/wiki/CNES "CNES")). It is operated from [Toulouse](/wiki/Toulouse "Toulouse").

## Ground segment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ground segment")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/DNO_DORIS.jpg/330px-DNO_DORIS.jpg)](/wiki/File:DNO_DORIS.jpg)

The UHF transmitting antenna of DORIS ground station at [Dionysos, Greece](/wiki/Dionysos,_Greece "Dionysos, Greece")

The [ground segment](/wiki/Ground_segment "Ground segment") includes about 50-60 [ground stations](/wiki/Ground_station "Ground station"), equally distributed over the [Earth](/wiki/Earth "Earth") and ensure a good coverage for orbit determination. For the installation of a beacon only electricity is required because the station only emits a signal but does not receive any information. DORIS beacons transmit to the satellites on two UHF frequencies, 401.25 MHz and 2036.25 MHz.

### Australian ground segments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Australian ground segments")]

There are two active DORIS [stations in Australia](/wiki/Earth_stations_in_Australia "Earth stations in Australia"):[[1]](#cite_note-1)

* [Yatharagga](/wiki/Yatharagga "Yatharagga") - active
* [Orroral Valley Tracking Station](/wiki/Orroral_Valley_Tracking_Station "Orroral Valley Tracking Station") - no longer active
* [Mount Stromlo Observatory](/wiki/Mount_Stromlo_Observatory "Mount Stromlo Observatory") - currently active, replaced Orroral Valley Tracking station installation

## Space segment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Space segment")]

The best known satellites equipped with DORIS receivers are the altimetry satellites [TOPEX/Poseidon](/wiki/TOPEX/Poseidon "TOPEX/Poseidon"), [Jason-1](/wiki/Jason-1 "Jason-1"), [OSTM/Jason-2](/wiki/OSTM/Jason-2 "OSTM/Jason-2"), [Jason-3](/wiki/Jason-3 "Jason-3"), and [Sentinel-6 Michael Freilich](/wiki/Sentinel-6_Michael_Freilich "Sentinel-6 Michael Freilich"). They are used to observe the ocean surface as well as currents or wave heights. DORIS contributes to their orbit accuracy of about 2 cm.

Other DORIS satellites are the [Envisat](/wiki/Envisat "Envisat"), [SPOT](/wiki/SPOT_(satellite) "SPOT (satellite)"), [HY-2A](/wiki/HY_(satellite) "HY (satellite)") and [CryoSat-2](/wiki/CryoSat-2 "CryoSat-2") satellites.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Positioning

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Positioning")]

Apart from orbit determination, the DORIS observations are used for positioning of ground stations. The accuracy is a bit lower than with [GPS](/wiki/GPS "GPS"), but it still contributes to the [International Terrestrial Reference Frame](/wiki/International_Terrestrial_Reference_Frame "International Terrestrial Reference Frame") (ITRF).[[3]](#cite_note-IGN2014-3)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Argos (satellite system)](/wiki/Argos_(satellite_system) "Argos (satellite system)")

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** Australia, c\=AU\;o\=Australia Government\;ou\=Geoscience (15 May 2014). ["Doppler Orbitography and Radiopositioning Integrated by Satellite"](http://www.ga.gov.au/scientific-topics/positioning-navigation/geodesy/geodetic-techniques/doris). *ga.gov.au*. Geoscience Australia. Retrieved 24 June 2017.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: multiple names: authors list ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_multiple_names:_authors_list "Category:CS1 maint: multiple names: authors list"))

3. **[^](#cite_ref-IGN2014_3-0)** ["ITRF2014"](http://itrf.ensg.ign.fr/ITRF_solutions/2014/ITRF2014.php). *itrf.ensg.ign.fr*. IGN.

## Further reading

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DORIS_(satellite_system)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Further reading")]

* G. Seeber: *Satellite Geodesy*. De Gruyter-Verlag, 2. Auflage (590 p.), Berlin 2003
